headline: UMMC may have violated federal privacy law after ransomware attack
summary: A ransomware attack crippled the state's largest hospital — and may have left it in violation of federal patient-privacy law.

headline: Nonprofit donation to Jackson police overlaps with chief's proposed perks
summary: A nonprofit's offer to give up to $100,000 to Jackson police closely tracked the perks proposed for the city's new chief.

headline: Analysis: Proposed Jackson police chief contract may violate state law
summary: A close reading of the proposed contract for Jackson's police chief found terms that may run afoul of state law.

headline: Jackson plastic surgeon surrenders medical license amid patient allegations
summary: A prominent Jackson plastic surgeon gave up his medical license as patients came forward with allegations of botched care.

headline: JXN Water hasn't inspected half of Jackson's fire hydrants
summary: Records showed the utility had not inspected half the city's hydrants as firefighters struggled to find working ones.

headline: The Detective vs. the DA
summary: A former detective disputes claims he withheld evidence in the attempted-murder case of a Black FedEx driver.

headline: 'Side Hustle': The jobs that cost a mayor and a police chief their offices
summary: A year-long look at side jobs held by a Richland mayor and a Pearl police chief forced both from office and prompted a state audit.

headline: Exclusive: Emails reveal staffing shortage threatened to shut down water plants
summary: Internal emails exposed staffing shortages that nearly shut Jackson's water plants — and led the city to fire a whistleblower.

headline: 'Under the Influence'
summary: A Rankin County official's drunken conduct in the hours before he faced a felony charge.

headline: Pandemic Profits: How Hinds Co. spent more than $1.8M in election grant funds
summary: An investigation into election grant spending that ended in three convictions and the removal of an election commissioner.

headline: City of Jackson slapped with largest fine in state history over records
summary: Jackson drew the largest Ethics Commission judgment in state history for failing to turn over public records to WLBT.

headline: 'Pattern of Secrecy': Misleading numbers behind Jackson's crime stats
summary: How the Jackson Police Department released misleading crime statistics — and fought to keep the real numbers secret.
